title: quercus sessilis var. longicarpa
intro: Quercus sessilis var. longicarpa, also known as the Long-fruited Chinquapin, is a variety of evergreen shrub or tree that belongs to the beech family (Fagaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating gastrointestinal issues, due to its high tannin and flavonoid content. The health benefits of Quercus sessilis var. longicarpa include its potential to act as an anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant agent, making it beneficial for immune system support and overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Quercus sessilis var. longicarpa include infusions and decoctions made from its bark, leaves, or seeds, although more research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy for human consumption.
